---
title: "Flow Metrics"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import json
from pathlib import Path
from IPython.display import HTML, display
import plotly.graph_objects as go

_candidates = [Path("quarto-data/flow-metrics.json"), Path("../quarto-data/flow-metrics.json")]
data_file = next((p for p in _candidates if p.exists()), None)
if data_file is None:
    raise FileNotFoundError(
        "quarto-data/flow-metrics.json not found. "
        "Run: python NceGitLab.py --report flow-metrics"
    )

d     = json.loads(data_file.read_text())
group = d["group"]
dist  = d["distribution"]
ft    = d["flow_time"]
```

```{python}
HTML(f"""
<p>
  <strong>Report Date:</strong> {d['report_date']} &nbsp;|&nbsp;
  <strong>Group:</strong> <a href="{group['url']}" target="_blank">{group['name']}</a>
</p>""")
```

## 📈 Flow Velocity — Delivered per PI

Features and Capabilities closed per PI. Consistent delivery of committed items is the primary ART health signal.

```{python}
rows = d["velocity"]
has_data = any(r["total"] > 0 for r in rows)

if not has_data:
    display(HTML(
        "<p><em>No closed epics found — velocity will populate as PIs complete.</em></p>"
    ))
else:
    piids = [r["piid"] for r in rows]
    feats = [r["features"]     for r in rows]
    caps  = [r["capabilities"] for r in rows]
    hover_f = [f"PI: {r['piid']}<br>Features delivered: {r['features']}" for r in rows]
    hover_c = [f"PI: {r['piid']}<br>Capabilities delivered: {r['capabilities']}" for r in rows]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name="Features",
        x=piids, y=feats,
        marker_color="#3b82f6",
        hovertext=hover_f, hoverinfo="text",
    ))
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name="Capabilities",
        x=piids, y=caps,
        marker_color="#10b981",
        hovertext=hover_c, hoverinfo="text",
    ))
    fig.update_layout(
        barmode="stack",
        xaxis_title="Program Increment",
        yaxis_title="Items Delivered",
        height=350,
        margin=dict(l=50, r=30, t=20, b=60),
        plot_bgcolor="#f9fafb",
        paper_bgcolor="white",
        legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.08),
        font=dict(size=13),
    )
    fig.show()
```

```{python}
if has_data:
    html = (
        '<table class="table table-sm table-bordered">'
        "<thead><tr><th>PI</th><th>Features</th><th>Capabilities</th><th>Total Delivered</th></tr></thead>"
        "<tbody>"
    )
    for r in rows:
        html += f'<tr><td><code>{r["piid"]}</code></td><td>{r["features"]}</td><td>{r["capabilities"]}</td><td><strong>{r["total"]}</strong></td></tr>'
    html += "</tbody></table>"
    display(HTML(html))
```

---

## 📦 Flow Load — Work in Progress

Open epics by PI. High WIP signals overloaded PIs and increases the risk of context-switching and delayed delivery.

```{python}
load_rows = d["load"]
no_pi     = d["load_no_pi"]

piids_l = [r["piid"] for r in load_rows]
feats_l = [r["features"]     for r in load_rows]
caps_l  = [r["capabilities"] for r in load_rows]
epics_l = [r["epics"]        for r in load_rows]

if no_pi:
    piids_l.append("(no PI)")
    feats_l.append(no_pi["features"])
    caps_l.append(no_pi["capabilities"])
    epics_l.append(no_pi["epics"])

hover_f2 = [f"PI: {p}<br>Open Features: {f}" for p, f in zip(piids_l, feats_l)]
hover_c2 = [f"PI: {p}<br>Open Capabilities: {c}" for p, c in zip(piids_l, caps_l)]
hover_e2 = [f"PI: {p}<br>Open Portfolio Epics: {e}" for p, e in zip(piids_l, epics_l)]

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(name="Features",     x=piids_l, y=feats_l, marker_color="#3b82f6",
                      hovertext=hover_f2, hoverinfo="text"))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(name="Capabilities", x=piids_l, y=caps_l,  marker_color="#10b981",
                      hovertext=hover_c2, hoverinfo="text"))
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(name="Epics",        x=piids_l, y=epics_l, marker_color="#f59e0b",
                      hovertext=hover_e2, hoverinfo="text"))
fig.update_layout(
    barmode="stack",
    xaxis_title="Program Increment",
    yaxis_title="Open Items (WIP)",
    height=350,
    margin=dict(l=50, r=30, t=20, b=60),
    plot_bgcolor="#f9fafb",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.08),
    font=dict(size=13),
)
fig.show()
```

```{python}
html = (
    '<table class="table table-sm table-bordered">'
    "<thead><tr><th>PI</th><th>Features</th><th>Capabilities</th><th>Epics</th><th>Total</th><th>Planned Weight</th></tr></thead>"
    "<tbody>"
)
for r in load_rows:
    html += (
        f'<tr><td><code>{r["piid"]}</code></td>'
        f'<td>{r["features"]}</td><td>{r["capabilities"]}</td>'
        f'<td>{r["epics"]}</td><td><strong>{r["total"]}</strong></td>'
        f'<td>{r["planned_weight"]:,}</td></tr>'
    )
if no_pi:
    html += (
        f'<tr><td><em>(no PI)</em></td>'
        f'<td>{no_pi["features"]}</td><td>{no_pi["capabilities"]}</td>'
        f'<td>{no_pi["epics"]}</td><td><strong>{no_pi["total"]}</strong></td>'
        f'<td>{no_pi["planned_weight"]:,}</td></tr>'
    )
html += "</tbody></table>"
display(HTML(html))
```

---

## 🔀 Flow Distribution — Work Type Mix

### By SAFe Hierarchy Level

```{python}
types  = [r["type"]  for r in dist["by_type"]]
counts = [r["count"] for r in dist["by_type"]]
colors = {"Feature": "#3b82f6", "Capability": "#10b981", "Epic": "#f59e0b"}
hovers = [
    f"{r['type']}<br>Count: {r['count']}<br>% Items: {r['pct_items']}<br>Planned Wt: {r['planned_weight']:,}<br>% Weight: {r['pct_weight']}"
    for r in dist["by_type"]
]

fig = go.Figure(go.Pie(
    labels=types,
    values=counts,
    hole=0.45,
    marker_colors=[colors.get(t, "#6b7280") for t in types],
    hovertext=hovers,
    hoverinfo="text+percent",
    textinfo="label+value",
))
fig.update_layout(
    height=320,
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=20, b=20),
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    showlegend=True,
    font=dict(size=13),
)
fig.show()
```

```{python}
html = (
    '<table class="table table-sm table-bordered">'
    "<thead><tr><th>Type</th><th>Count</th><th>% Items</th><th>Planned Weight</th><th>% Weight</th></tr></thead>"
    "<tbody>"
)
for r in dist["by_type"]:
    html += (
        f'<tr><td>{r["type"]}</td><td>{r["count"]}</td>'
        f'<td>{r["pct_items"]}</td><td>{r["planned_weight"]:,}</td>'
        f'<td>{r["pct_weight"]}</td></tr>'
    )
html += (
    f'<tr><td><strong>Total</strong></td>'
    f'<td><strong>{dist["total_epics"]}</strong></td>'
    f'<td></td>'
    f'<td><strong>{dist["total_planned_weight"]:,}</strong></td>'
    f'<td></td></tr>'
)
html += "</tbody></table>"
display(HTML(html))
```

### By Work Type Label

```{python}
if not dist["has_work_type_labels"]:
    display(HTML(
        "<p><em>No <code>type::</code> labels found. Apply "
        "<code>type::feature</code>, <code>type::enabler</code>, "
        "<code>type::infrastructure</code>, or <code>type::defect</code> "
        "labels to epics to enable this view.</em></p>"
        "<table class='table table-sm table-bordered'>"
        "<thead><tr><th>Work Type</th><th>SAFe 6.0 Target</th></tr></thead>"
        "<tbody>"
        "<tr><td>Features (business value)</td><td>~50%</td></tr>"
        "<tr><td>Enablers (tech / architecture)</td><td>~30%</td></tr>"
        "<tr><td>Infrastructure / DevSecOps</td><td>~20%</td></tr>"
        "<tr><td>Defects</td><td>minimize</td></tr>"
        "</tbody></table>"
    ))
else:
    wt_labels  = [r["label"] for r in dist["by_work_type"]]
    wt_counts  = [r["count"] for r in dist["by_work_type"]]
    wt_targets = {"type::feature": 50, "type::enabler": 30, "type::infrastructure": 20, "type::defect": 0}
    wt_hovers  = [
        f"{r['label']}<br>Count: {r['count']}<br>% Labelled: {r['pct_labelled']}<br>SAFe Target: {r['safe_target']}"
        for r in dist["by_work_type"]
    ]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        name="Actual",
        x=wt_labels, y=wt_counts,
        marker_color="#3b82f6",
        hovertext=wt_hovers, hoverinfo="text",
    ))
    # SAFe target reference — use total labelled items as denominator
    total_lab = sum(wt_counts)
    if total_lab > 0:
        target_vals = [round(wt_targets.get(l, 0) / 100 * total_lab) for l in wt_labels]
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            name="SAFe Target",
            x=wt_labels, y=target_vals,
            mode="markers",
            marker=dict(symbol="line-ew", size=20, color="#ef4444",
                        line=dict(width=3, color="#ef4444")),
            hoverinfo="skip",
        ))
    fig.update_layout(
        yaxis_title="Items",
        height=320,
        margin=dict(l=50, r=30, t=20, b=80),
        plot_bgcolor="#f9fafb",
        paper_bgcolor="white",
        legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.08),
        font=dict(size=13),
    )
    fig.show()

    html = (
        '<table class="table table-sm table-bordered">'
        "<thead><tr><th>Work Type</th><th>Count</th><th>% of Labelled</th><th>SAFe Target</th></tr></thead>"
        "<tbody>"
    )
    for r in dist["by_work_type"]:
        html += (
            f'<tr><td><code>{r["label"]}</code></td>'
            f'<td>{r["count"]}</td><td>{r["pct_labelled"]}</td>'
            f'<td>{r["safe_target"]}</td></tr>'
        )
    if dist["unlabeled_count"]:
        html += f'<tr><td><em>(unlabelled)</em></td><td>{dist["unlabeled_count"]}</td><td>—</td><td>—</td></tr>'
    html += "</tbody></table>"
    display(HTML(html))
```

---

## ⏱ Flow Time — Cycle Time Analysis

How long epics spend in-flight. Shorter, more consistent cycle times indicate a healthy flow system.

### Age of Open Epics (days since created)

```{python}
html = (
    '<table class="table table-sm table-bordered">'
    "<thead><tr><th>Type</th><th>Count</th><th>Avg Age</th><th>Min</th><th>Max</th></tr></thead>"
    "<tbody>"
)
for r in ft["open_ages"]:
    avg = f'{r["avg_days"]} days' if r["avg_days"] is not None else "—"
    mn  = r["min_days"] if r["min_days"] is not None else "—"
    mx  = r["max_days"] if r["max_days"] is not None else "—"
    html += f'<tr><td>{r["type"]}</td><td>{r["count"]}</td><td>{avg}</td><td>{mn}</td><td>{mx}</td></tr>'
html += "</tbody></table>"
display(HTML(html))
```

### Cycle Time — Closed Epics _(updated_at used as close_date proxy)_

```{python}
if not ft["has_closed_data"]:
    display(HTML("<p><em>No closed epics — cycle time will populate as work completes.</em></p>"))
else:
    html = (
        '<table class="table table-sm table-bordered">'
        "<thead><tr><th>Type</th><th>Count</th><th>Avg Cycle Time</th><th>Min</th><th>Max</th></tr></thead>"
        "<tbody>"
    )
    for r in ft["closed_cycles"]:
        avg = f'{r["avg_days"]} days' if r["avg_days"] is not None else "—"
        mn  = r["min_days"] if r["min_days"] is not None else "—"
        mx  = r["max_days"] if r["max_days"] is not None else "—"
        html += f'<tr><td>{r["type"]}</td><td>{r["count"]}</td><td>{avg}</td><td>{mn}</td><td>{mx}</td></tr>'
    html += "</tbody></table>"
    display(HTML(html))
```

---

## 🎯 Flow Predictability

Percentage of PI objectives met as committed. SAFe guidance: 80–100% is the healthy range. Consistently at 100% may indicate sandbagging.

```{python}
pred_rows = d["predictability"]

if not pred_rows:
    display(HTML("<p><em>No PI-committed Features or Capabilities found.</em></p>"))
else:
    piids_p  = [r["piid"] for r in pred_rows]
    pcts     = [r["pct"]  for r in pred_rows]
    bar_colors = [
        "#22c55e" if p >= 80 else ("#f59e0b" if p >= 60 else "#ef4444")
        for p in pcts
    ]
    hovers_p = [
        f"PI: {r['piid']}<br>Committed: {r['committed']}<br>Delivered: {r['delivered']}<br>Predictability: {r['pct']}%"
        for r in pred_rows
    ]

    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
        x=piids_p, y=pcts,
        marker_color=bar_colors,
        hovertext=hovers_p, hoverinfo="text",
        name="Predictability %",
    ))
    # SAFe healthy range: 80% lower bound
    fig.add_hline(y=80, line_dash="dash", line_color="#22c55e",
                  annotation_text="80% target", annotation_position="top right")
    fig.add_hline(y=60, line_dash="dot", line_color="#f59e0b",
                  annotation_text="60% floor", annotation_position="bottom right")
    fig.update_layout(
        xaxis_title="Program Increment",
        yaxis_title="Predictability %",
        yaxis_range=[0, 110],
        height=350,
        margin=dict(l=50, r=30, t=20, b=60),
        plot_bgcolor="#f9fafb",
        paper_bgcolor="white",
        showlegend=False,
        font=dict(size=13),
    )
    fig.show()

    html = (
        '<table class="table table-sm table-bordered">'
        "<thead><tr><th>PI</th><th>Committed</th><th>Delivered</th><th>Predictability</th></tr></thead>"
        "<tbody>"
    )
    for r in pred_rows:
        html += (
            f'<tr><td><code>{r["piid"]}</code></td>'
            f'<td>{r["committed"]}</td><td>{r["delivered"]}</td>'
            f'<td>{r["icon"]} {r["pct"]}%</td></tr>'
        )
    html += "</tbody></table>"
    display(HTML(html))
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## About Flow Metrics

SAFe 6.0 flow metrics measure *how efficiently* work moves through the portfolio.

| Metric | This Report | Status |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| Flow Velocity | Delivered epics per PI | ✅ |
| Flow Load | Open epics per PI (WIP) | ✅ |
| Flow Distribution | Work type mix | ✅ |
| Flow Time | Cycle time (open age + closed proxy) | ✅ |
| Flow Predictability | % PI objectives met | ✅ |
| Flow Efficiency | Value-added vs wait time | ⬜ Requires time tracking |
:::
